_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved.

activity_name: Pool equipment install (pump, filter, heater, chlorinator, controller)

hazards.title: Electric shock — final connections
hazards.risk: Final connections of pump, heater, chlorinator to circuits

controls.title: Final electrical connection by licensed electrician only

hazards.title: Gas leak — gas heater install
hazards.risk: Gas heater connection — leak / explosion if not gas-fit

controls.title: Gas connection by licensed gasfitter only; Type A certificate issued
Leak test all joints with soap solution; pressure decay test

hazards.title: Manual handling — pump / filter / heater
hazards.risk: Pump pots and heaters typically 30–80kg; awkward into equipment bay

controls.title: Trolley or two-person lift for all units > 20kg

hazards.title: PVC primer / solvent fumes in equipment bay
hazards.risk: Concentrated fumes in enclosed equipment bay during connections

controls.title: Maintain ventilation; outdoor-rated joints made outside where possible

tasks: Pre-start: equipment delivered, inspected for transit damage, all components present per spec.
Lay out equipment per manufacturer's clearance requirements; serviceability access maintained.
Bolt down pump pot; level filter and heater per manufacturer.
Plumb in suction, return, backwash, and chlorinator lines; PVC unions for serviceability.
Gasfitter: connect gas heater; pressure / leak test; Type A certificate.
Electrician: connect all final circuits; insulation + bonding tests; Electrical CoC.
Install controller; configure per owner preferences.
Label all valves, isolators, and circuits clearly.
Operating manual + warranties uploaded to Hub.
